docs.title: Visão Geral
docs.text:
A Beverage Sales API é uma aplicação baseada em FastAPI para expor um serviço de inferência de machine learning com foco em previsão de vendas e sinais de anomalia.

Com base nos arquivos enviados, a API utiliza:

  • um modelo principal de predição carregado com joblib
  • um modelo de anomalia baseado em Isolation Forest
  • metadados do modelo carregados de um arquivo JSON
  • dados históricos em formato parquet
  • uma etapa de engenharia de atributos para preparar a entrada antes da predição

docs.title: Objetivo da API
docs.text:
O objetivo é receber uma nova observação de venda, localizar um histórico compatível por Product e Region, gerar features com base nesse histórico, calcular sinais de anomalia e então produzir a predição final.

Em termos práticos, a API segue uma arquitetura de inferência híbrida:

  1. valida o payload recebido
  2. carrega o histórico de referência
  3. gera features temporais e agregadas
  4. calcula variáveis auxiliares para anomalia
  5. executa o modelo final de predição
  6. devolve o resultado em JSON

docs.title: Parquet file
docs.text:
In the Beverage-Sales project, the notebook 04_anomaly_detection.ipynb generates the compressed Parquet file: 📦 anomaly_predictions_api.parquet

Inside the API repository, this file must be placed in the following directory: app/data

To ensure the API can access this data, references need to be updated in two configuration files (which has already been set up in this version): 🛠️ app/src/config/src_config.py 🛠️ app/core/config.py --- --- --- No projeto Beverage-Sales, o notebook 04_anomaly_detection.ipynb gera o arquivo Parquet compactado: 📦 anomaly_predictions_api.parquet

No projeto da API, este arquivo deve ser armazenado no diretório: app/data

Para que a API consuma esses dados, é necessário atualizar o caminho em dois arquivos de configuração (o que já foi implementado nesta versão): 🛠️ app/src/config/src_config.py 🛠️ app/core/config.py

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Endpoint simples para verificação de disponibilidade da API.

Resposta esperada:

{
  "status": "ok"
}

docs.title: POST /login
docs.text:
Endpoint de autenticação.

Recebe:

  • username
  • password

Se as credenciais forem válidas, retorna um token de acesso do tipo bearer.

Resposta esperada:

{
  "access_token": "<token>",
  "token_type": "bearer"
}

docs.title: POST /predict
docs.text:
Endpoint principal de predição.

Este endpoint exige autenticação e recebe um payload com dados de pedido, cliente, produto, preço, quantidade, desconto, região e data do pedido.

Campos esperados:

  • Order_ID
  • Customer_ID
  • Customer_Type
  • Product
  • Category
  • Unit_Price
  • Quantity
  • Discount
  • Total_Price
  • Region
  • Order_Date

docs.title: Fluxo interno do endpoint /predict
docs.text:
1. O payload é convertido para DataFrame.
  2. Os campos numéricos e de data são normalizados.
  3. O histórico parquet é carregado.
  4. O histórico é filtrado por Product e Region.
  5. Os dados históricos e o novo registro são combinados.
  6. A classe DataQualityFeatures gera as features.
  7. A linha correspondente à data de predição é selecionada.
  8. São criadas features auxiliares para o modelo de anomalia.
  9. O Isolation Forest gera:
  10. anomaly_flag
  11. anomaly_score
  12. anomaly_label
  13. O modelo LightGBM executa a predição final.
  14. A API retorna o valor previsto e informações adicionais.

docs.title: Exemplo de resposta
docs.text:
{
  "status": "success",
  "prediction": 134.27,
  "target": "quantity_sum",
  "model_version": "v1",
  "model_name": "Beverage Sales Hybrid Forecasting Model",
  "anomaly": {
    "anomaly_flag": 0,
    "anomaly_score": 0.1842,
    "anomaly_label": "Normal"
  },
  "input_reference": {
    "Order_ID": "ORD-1001",
    "Product": "Cola 350ml",
    "Region": "Baden-Württemberg",
    "Order_Date": "2023-11-10"
  }
}

Retorna:

docs.text:
Executa uma validação completa dos recursos principais da API.

Verifica ao mesmo tempo:

  • modelo LightGBM
  • modelo de anomalia

docs.title: Dependências Funcionais Observadas
docs.text:
Com base nos arquivos enviados, a API depende de alguns componentes internos que não foram incluídos no material:

  • app.core.security
  • app.src.config.src_config
  • app.src.features.data_quality_features

Isso significa que parte do comportamento foi documentada com base no uso observado no código.

docs.title: Recursos de Arquivo Esperados
docs.text:
A API espera a existência de arquivos locais, com paths vindos de src_config, como por exemplo:

  • arquivo do modelo LightGBM
  • arquivo do modelo de anomalia
  • arquivo de metadados JSON
  • parquet histórico

Sem esses arquivos, a API pode responder com erro interno.

docs.title: Tratamento de Erros
docs.text:
O endpoint /predict trata os seguintes cenários:

  • erro de validação de dados → HTTP 400
  • arquivo ausente → HTTP 500
  • erro inesperado → HTTP 500

Além disso, se não houver histórico para o par Product + Region, a API retorna erro de validação.

docs.title: Arquitetura Lógica Resumida
docs.text:
Cliente
  ↓
/login
  ↓
Token Bearer
  ↓
/predict
  ↓
Validação do payload
  ↓
Carga do histórico parquet
  ↓
Geração de features
  ↓
Cálculo de anomalia
  ↓
Predição com LightGBM
  ↓
Resposta JSON

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
Deploy e Infraestrutura A solução foi containerizada utilizando Docker. Devido à natureza do modelo (série temporal com dependência de histórico e geração de sliding window), a aplicação exige persistência de dados. Em um cenário de produção, o ideal seria utilizar um banco de dados dedicado em um container à parte; contudo, para simplificar a arquitetura deste portfólio, optou-se pelo uso de um arquivo Parquet.

Fluxo de Dados e Configuração O arquivo compactado 📦 anomaly_predictions_api.parquet é gerado previamente no projeto Beverage-Sales através do notebook 04_anomaly_detection.ipynb.

Para o funcionamento correto da API, siga as instruções abaixo:

Armazenamento: Certifique-se de que o arquivo gerado está mapeado no diretório: app/data/anomaly_predictions_api.parquet

Configuração de Caminhos: Para que a API consuma esses dados, os caminhos de leitura já mapeiam esse diretório nos arquivos de configuração do projeto (implementado por padrão nesta versão): 🛠️ app/src/config/src_config.py 🛠️ app/core/config.py

docs.title: dockerCommpose
docs.text:
Utilize o arquivo, anexo ao projeto: docker-compose.yml

A imagem abaixo demonstra o uso. --- ---

docs.title: swagger
docs.text: http://localhost:8062/docs

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Beverage Sales API is a FastAPI application designed to expose a machine learning inference service for sales prediction with anomaly-aware logic.

Based on the provided files, the API uses:

  • a prediction model loaded with joblib
  • an anomaly detection model based on Isolation Forest
  • model metadata loaded from JSON
  • historical reference data stored in parquet format
  • a feature engineering step before the final inference

docs.title: Main Objective
docs.text:
The API receives a new sales-related record, finds compatible historical data by Product and Region, creates derived features, adds anomaly signals, and returns the final prediction.

This creates a hybrid inference flow with:

  1. payload validation
  2. historical reference loading
  3. feature generation
  4. anomaly signal generation
  5. final prediction
  6. structured JSON response

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Simple health check endpoint.

Response

{
  "status": "ok"
}

docs.title: POST /login
docs.text:
Authentication endpoint.

It receives:

  • username
  • password

If authentication is successful, it returns a bearer token.

Response

{
  "access_token": "<token>",
  "token_type": "bearer"
}

docs.title: POST /predict
docs.text:
Main secured prediction endpoint.

This route requires authentication and receives a structured sales request with order, customer, product, pricing, quantity, region, and date data.

Expected fields:

  • Order_ID
  • Customer_ID
  • Customer_Type
  • Product
  • Category
  • Unit_Price
  • Quantity
  • Discount
  • Total_Price
  • Region
  • Order_Date

docs.title: Internal Prediction Flow
docs.text:
1. The request payload is converted into a pandas DataFrame.
  2. Numeric and date fields are normalized.
  3. Historical parquet data is loaded.
  4. The reference history is filtered by Product and Region.
  5. Historical data and the new record are concatenated.
  6. DataQualityFeatures is used to build the derived features.
  7. The feature row for the prediction date is selected.
  8. Auxiliary anomaly features are created.
  9. The Isolation Forest model generates:
  10. anomaly_flag
  11. anomaly_score
  12. anomaly_label
  13. The LightGBM model performs the final prediction.
  14. The API returns the response in JSON format.

docs.title: Example Prediction Response
docs.text:
{
  "status": "success",
  "prediction": 134.27,
  "target": "quantity_sum",
  "model_version": "v1",
  "model_name": "Beverage Sales Hybrid Forecasting Model",
  "anomaly": {
    "anomaly_flag": 0,
    "anomaly_score": 0.1842,
    "anomaly_label": "Normal"
  },
  "input_reference": {
    "Order_ID": "ORD-1001",
    "Product": "Cola 350ml",
    "Region": "Baden-Württemberg",
    "Order_Date": "2023-11-10"
  }
}

docs.title: Debug Endpoints
docs.text: The uploaded files also expose a debug router for environment validation and resource inspection.

docs.title: GET /debug/paths
docs.text:
Checks whether the main project files exist.

It validates:

  • metadata file
  • parquet file
  • LightGBM model file
  • anomaly model file

docs.title: GET /debug/metadata
docs.text:
Loads and summarizes the metadata file.

Returns values such as:

  • status
  • metadata keys
  • target
  • model version
  • number of raw features
  • number of model features

docs.title: GET /debug/parquet-sample
docs.text:
Loads a small sample from the parquet dataset.

Returns:

  • status
  • row count
  • column list
  • sample records

docs.title: GET /debug/models
docs.text:
Checks whether the models can be loaded correctly.

Returns the Python object types for:

  • LightGBM model
  • anomaly model

docs.title: GET /debug/validate-schema
docs.text:
Compares parquet columns with the expected raw and model features from metadata.

Returns:

  • parquet column count
  • raw feature count
  • model feature count
  • missing raw features
  • missing model features

docs.title: GET /debug/full-check
docs.text:
Runs a combined validation for all major resources.

It attempts to load:

  • metadata
  • parquet data
  • LightGBM model
  • anomaly model

docs.title: Referenced Internal Modules
docs.text:
Some modules are referenced in the uploaded files but were not included in the shared material:

  • app.core.security
  • app.src.config.src_config
  • app.src.features.data_quality_features

Because of that, part of this documentation was inferred from the way these modules are used in the routes.

docs.title: Expected Local Resources
docs.text:
The API depends on local files configured through src_config, including:

  • LightGBM model file
  • Isolation Forest model file
  • metadata JSON file
  • historical parquet dataset

If any of these resources is missing, the API may return internal errors.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The /predict endpoint explicitly handles:

  • invalid input or business validation problems → HTTP 400
  • missing required files → HTTP 500
  • unexpected internal failures → HTTP 500

The route also returns a validation error when no historical data is found for the same Product and Region.

docs.title: Logical Architecture
docs.text:
Client
  ↓
/login
  ↓
Bearer Token
  ↓
/predict
  ↓
Payload validation
  ↓
Historical parquet load
  ↓
Feature engineering
  ↓
Anomaly calculation
  ↓
LightGBM inference
  ↓
JSON response

docs.title: Endpoint Details
docs.text: This document describes the endpoints identified in the uploaded FastAPI route files for the Beverage Sales API.

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Checks whether the API is running.

Purpose - quick operational validation - useful for containers, probes, and monitoring

Success response

{
  "status": "ok"
}

Authentication required - No

docs.title: POST /login
docs.text:
Authenticates a user and returns an access token.

Request body

{
  "username": "your_user",
  "password": "your_password"
}

Success response

{
  "access_token": "<token>",
  "token_type": "bearer"
}

Error response - 401 Unauthorized when credentials are invalid

Authentication required - No

Notes - The token is created with an expiration window of 60 minutes according to the uploaded code. - The implementation depends on internal security functions: - authenticate_user - create_access_token

docs.title: POST /predict
docs.text:
Runs the main prediction flow using historical data, derived features, anomaly signals, and the final LightGBM model.

Authentication required - Yes

The route uses a dependency called get_current_user, so the request must include a valid bearer token.

docs.title: Request body
docs.text:
{
  "Order_ID": "ORD-1001",
  "Customer_ID": "CUST-10",
  "Customer_Type": "Retail",
  "Product": "Cola 350ml",
  "Category": "Soft Drinks",
  "Unit_Price": 4.50,
  "Quantity": 20,
  "Discount": 0.10,
  "Total_Price": 81.00,
  "Region": "Baden-Württemberg",
  "Order_Date": "2023-11-10"
}

docs.title: Field description
docs.text: Field Type Description Order_ID string Order identifier Customer_ID string Customer identifier Customer_Type string Customer segment or type Product string Product name Category string Product category Unit_Price float Unit price value Quantity float Quantity value Discount float Discount value Total_Price float Total order price Region string Sales region Order_Date string Order date, converted to datetime

docs.title: Internal processing steps
docs.text:
The uploaded code shows the following execution path:

  1. request payload is validated by PredictRequest
  2. payload is converted to a DataFrame
  3. numeric fields are coerced to numeric values
  4. Order_Date is parsed as datetime
  5. parquet historical data is loaded
  6. history is filtered by Product and Region
  7. historical data is combined with the new row
  8. DataQualityFeatures builds the feature set
  9. the row for the requested prediction date is selected
  10. anomaly helper features are created:
  11. if_qty_signal
  12. if_sales_signal
  13. if_discount_signal
  14. if_ticket_signal
  15. Isolation Forest generates:
  16. anomaly_flag
  17. anomaly_label
  18. anomaly_score
  19. LightGBM predicts the final output

docs.title: Success response example
docs.text:
{
  "status": "success",
  "prediction": 134.27,
  "target": "quantity_sum",
  "model_version": "v1",
  "model_name": "Beverage Sales Hybrid Forecasting Model",
  "anomaly": {
    "anomaly_flag": 0,
    "anomaly_score": 0.1842,
    "anomaly_label": "Normal"
  },
  "input_reference": {
    "Order_ID": "ORD-1001",
    "Product": "Cola 350ml",
    "Region": "Baden-Württemberg",
    "Order_Date": "2023-11-10"
  }
}

docs.title: 400 Bad Request
docs.text:
Returned for validation or business-rule problems, such as:

  • invalid Order_Date
  • missing compatible history for the same Product and Region
  • failure to build the prediction feature row

docs.title: 500 Internal Server Error
docs.text:
Returned for problems such as:

  • missing model file
  • missing parquet file
  • missing metadata file
  • unexpected internal exception

docs.title: 4. Debug Endpoints
docs.text: These endpoints help validate local resources and inspect the project state.

docs.title: GET /debug/paths
docs.text:
Checks file existence for key resources.

Returns - metadata path and existence - parquet path and existence - LightGBM model path and existence - anomaly model path and existence

Authentication required - Not shown in the uploaded code

docs.title: GET /debug/metadata
docs.text:
Loads metadata JSON and returns a summary.

Returns - status - metadata keys - target - model version - raw feature count - model feature count

Authentication required - Not shown in the uploaded code

docs.title: GET /debug/parquet-sample
docs.text:
Loads a small parquet sample.

Returns - status - row count - column names - sample records

Authentication required - Not shown in the uploaded code

docs.title: GET /debug/models
docs.text:
Attempts to load both model files.

Returns - status - LightGBM model Python type - anomaly model Python type

Authentication required - Not shown in the uploaded code

docs.title: GET /debug/validate-schema
docs.text:
Compares metadata feature definitions against parquet columns.

Returns - parquet column count - raw feature count - model feature count - missing raw features - missing model features

Authentication required - Not shown in the uploaded code

docs.title: GET /debug/full-check
docs.text:
Runs a combined validation of metadata, parquet, and models.

Returns - status - resource load flags - metadata keys - sample columns - sample record - model object types

Authentication required - Not shown in the uploaded code
